label: Transparent sea bass profile motif
prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: Morphous Sea Bass transparent motif chroma-key source
Primary request: Create only a realistic sea bass fish profile for a design-system catalog, isolated as a single complete subject on a removable chroma-key background.
Subject: An accurate European sea bass / branzino in clean side profile, full body visible, elongated streamlined shape, tapered head, spiny dorsal fin and soft rear dorsal fin, forked tail, small dark eye, reflective silver scales, olive-slate back, pearl belly, subtle blue-gray shadow along the lateral line, fine scale texture and restrained coastal sheen. The identity must read as sea bass, not salmon, tuna, sardine, trout, or generic aquarium fish.
Composition/framing: centered complete cutout with generous padding; include the entire fish, all fins, nose, tail, and silhouette without cropping. Face points right. Clean studio light preserving silver, olive, blue shadow, and pearlescent scale details. No cast shadow.
Transparent workflow: place the fish on a perfectly flat solid #ff00ff chroma-key background for background removal. The background must be one uniform color with no shadows, gradients, texture, reflections, floor plane, lighting variation, or texture. Do not use #ff00ff anywhere in the fish.
Constraints: no rocky coast scene, no water background, no plate, no dining props, no bubbles, no second fish, no text, no watermark, no logo, no frame.

label: Light-mode design-system board
prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous Sea Bass light-mode design-system board
Primary request: Generate one comprehensive light-mode design-system board for a Morphous theme named "Morphous Sea Bass", derived from a realistic right-facing sea bass fish cutout with reflective silver scales, an olive-slate back, pearl belly, subtle blue-gray lateral shadow, spiny dorsal fin, and rocky-coast context. This board is a source reference for implementing a clean dining system: reservations, menu operations, coastal restaurant service, inventory, and table dashboards.
Canvas: exact wide 16:9 design-system presentation, intended final asset 3840 x 2160. Make it feel like a polished 4K product design board with large readable labels, precise grid, and no tiny text.
Required visible sections: Morphous Sea Bass, Sea Bass Scales, Palette, Typography, Japanese Typography, Components, Navigation, Dining Dashboard / Data, Forms, Table, Command / Search, Status States, Texture, Radius, Spacing, Shadow, Tokens, Generated Asset Examples.
Reference use: Extract colors and interaction language from sea bass scales and rocky coast: polished silver, pearl white, olive slate, blue-gray shadow, graphite fin lines, wet stone gray, deep tide navy, sea glass blue, warm dining cream, and a restrained roe amber for warnings. The board must read as sea bass scales and clean rocky-coast dining, not a generic restaurant template.
Typography: English dining operations UI examples with Japanese typography guidance; use short readable labels such as Geist, Display, Body, Label, Numbers, Noto Sans JP, 日本語 UI, 見出し, 本文, ラベル, 数値. Use tabular numbers for reservations, covers, ticket times, and inventory metrics.
UI coverage: primary/secondary/ghost/destructive buttons, hover/pressed/disabled/loading states, icon buttons, inputs, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch, segmented control, tabs, badges, alerts, cards, metric cards, dining navigation sidebar, top nav, breadcrumb, table rows, data filters, command search palette, date picker, pagination, empty/loading/error/success states, progress, stepper, tooltip, dropdown menu, line chart, bar chart, donut chart, area chart, spacing scale, radius samples, border tokens, shadow tokens, texture samples, small icon set, token swatches.
Charts: include clear line, bar, donut, and area charts using silver olive blue shadow colors, with labels for Covers, Revenue, Waste, Prep, and Tickets.
Style: clean dining system, crisp shadcn/tweakcn product UI, restrained 8px card radius, bright pearl and warm dining surfaces, olive-slate primary actions, sea-glass blue focus rings, graphite separators inspired by fin rays, subtle scale-dot texture, rocky coast stone texture samples, polished menus, reservation cards, kitchen ticket cards, dense but calm dashboards.
Color palette: pearl white, polished silver, olive slate, blue-gray shadow, wet stone gray, graphite, sea glass blue, deep tide navy, warm dining cream, roe amber. Avoid unrelated colors.
Text: short readable interface labels only; section headings should be large and legible.
Constraints: no generic purple or saturated blue gradient dominance, no neon, no stock food photography, no plate hero photo, no extra animals, no random brand names, no watermark, no misspelling of "Morphous Sea Bass", no tiny unreadable paragraphs.

label: Dark-mode design-system board
prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous Sea Bass dark-mode design-system board
Primary request: Generate one comprehensive dark-mode design-system board for the Morphous theme named "Morphous Sea Bass" / "Sea Bass Scales". Translate the same light-mode clean dining system into dark mode; do not invent a new unrelated theme. It remains a refined coastal restaurant operations system for reservations, menu operations, inventory, kitchen tickets, and table dashboards.
Canvas: exact wide 16:9 design-system presentation, intended final asset 3840 x 2160. Make it feel like a polished 4K product design board with large readable labels, precise grid, and no tiny text.
Required visible sections: Morphous Sea Bass, Sea Bass Scales, Dark Mode, Palette, Typography, Japanese Typography, Components, Navigation, Dining Dashboard / Data, Forms, Table, Command / Search, Status States, Texture, Radius, Spacing, Shadow, Tokens, Generated Asset Examples.
Reference use: Preserve the sea bass scale identity and clean dining language: reflective silver highlights, olive-slate primary actions, blue-gray lateral shadow, graphite fin-ray dividers, wet stone panels, deep tide navy background, sea-glass blue focus rings, pearl text, warm dining cream surfaces, restrained roe amber warning accents. The board must read as sea bass scales and rocky-coast dining, not generic dark restaurant UI.
Typography: English dining operations UI examples with Japanese typography guidance; use short readable labels such as Geist, Display, Body, Label, Numbers, Noto Sans JP, 日本語 UI, 見出し, 本文, ラベル, 数値. Use tabular numbers for reservations, covers, ticket times, and inventory metrics.
UI coverage: primary/secondary/ghost/destructive buttons, hover/pressed/disabled/loading states, icon buttons, inputs, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch, segmented control, tabs, badges, alerts, cards, metric cards, dining navigation sidebar, top nav, breadcrumb, table rows, data filters, command search palette, date picker, pagination, empty/loading/error/success states, progress, stepper, tooltip, dropdown menu, line chart, bar chart, donut chart, area chart, spacing scale, radius samples, border tokens, shadow tokens, texture samples, small icon set, token swatches.
Charts: include clear line, bar, donut, and area charts using olive slate, sea glass blue, silver, graphite, and roe amber, with labels for Covers, Revenue, Waste, Prep, and Tickets.
Visual details: deep tide navy and graphite background, blue-black wet stone cards, olive-slate active rails, sea-glass focus rings, pearl and polished-silver text, amber warnings, subtle fish-scale dot texture, fin-ray line texture, rocky coast stone texture samples, crisp shadcn/tweakcn controls, 8px card radius, restrained contrast, operational dining dashboard density.
Color palette: deep tide navy, graphite, wet stone gray, olive slate, sea glass blue, blue-gray shadow, polished silver, pearl white, warm dining cream, roe amber. Avoid unrelated colors.
Text: short readable interface labels only; section headings should be large and legible.
Constraints: no neon nightclub look, no pure black flat background, no generic purple or saturated blue gradient dominance, no gaming UI, no stock food photography, no plate hero photo, no extra animals, no random brand names, no watermark, no misspelling of "Morphous Sea Bass", no tiny unreadable paragraphs.
